lede: A look at August 3 through the almanac — historic events, famous birthdays including Konstantin Melnikov and Frederick William III, and the day's notable deaths and observances.

events.title: Senegal
events.desc: Senegalese opposition parties, under the leadership of Mamadou Dia, launch the Antiimperialist Action Front – Suxxali Reew Mi.

events.title: Tandy Corporation
events.desc: Tandy Corporation announces the TRS-80, one of the world's first mass-produced personal computers.

events.title: Boeing 707
events.desc: A privately chartered Boeing 707 strikes a mountain peak and crashes near Agadir, Morocco, killing 188.

events.title: World War II
events.desc: World War II: Italian forces begin the invasion of British Somaliland.

events.title: World War I
events.desc: World War I: Germany declares war against France, while Romania declares its neutrality.

events.title: Internal Macedonian Revolutionary Organization
events.desc: Macedonian rebels in Kruševo proclaim the Kruševo Republic, which exists for only ten days before Ottoman Turks lay waste to the town.

events.title: La Scala
events.desc: The theatre La Scala in Milan is inaugurated with the première of Antonio Salieri's Europa riconosciuta.

events.title: Thirty Years' War
events.desc: Thirty Years' War: The Second Battle of Nördlingen sees French forces defeating those of the Holy Roman Empire.

events.title: Pope Stephen IX
events.desc: Frederick of Lorraine elected as Pope Stephen IX.

births.name: Konstantin Melnikov
births.line: Russian architect, designed the Rusakov Workers' Club

births.name: John Landis
births.line: American actor, director, producer, and screenwriter

births.name: Jean-Pierre Raffarin
births.line: French lawyer and politician, 166th Prime Minister of France

deaths.text: Aleksandr Solzhenitsyn, Russian novelist, dramatist and historian, Nobel Prize laureate
Konstantin Rokossovsky, Marshal of the Soviet Union during World War II
Ferdinand Georg Frobenius, German mathematician and academic

observances.text: Christian feast day: George Freeman Bragg, W. E. B. Du Bois (Episcopal Church)
Christian feast day: Olaf II of Norway (Translation of the relic)
Christian feast day: Stephen (Discovery of the relic)
